task: Built autonomous session infrastructure with multi-session persistence, context-aware trigger lifecycle, quota notification split, and per-session scratch handovers; identified and began addressing structured event logging gap needed for overnight autonomous run visualization.
discussion_summary: Session 1 diagnosed the missing structured event log as a blocker for autonomous demo visualization and mapped all existing logging locations. This session completed the infrastructure fixes (quota split, session_first_seen persistence, scratch handover cleanup, read-only Bash exemption, shlex tokenization) and committed them. The critical next step is building the structured JSONL event log (askr/state/events.jsonl) to record trigger_fired, companion_spawned, session_ended events with full lineage metadata before the overnight autonomous run, enabling credible session tree visualization and multi-session persistence story.

accomplishments.what: Split quota notification into three independent phases (silent poll → user notification → reset wait) to prevent interruption at 90% threshold when users can work to genuine quota edge
Persisted session_first_seen to disk (alongside trigger_state, companioned_sessions) to prevent grace period reset on daemon restart
Implemented per-session scratch handovers with auto-deletion after checkpoint merge to prevent accumulation and accidental commits
Exempted read-only Bash commands (cat, ls, tail, grep) from cross-repo block guard while maintaining write/edit security
Replaced raw regex split with shlex tokenization for Bash command parsing to honor quoted strings and complex patterns
Raised CONTEXT_TRIGGER from 0.60 to 0.70 and QUOTA_HIGH from 85 to 70 for aligned thresholds and more runway before companion spawn
Committed all lifecycle.py, checkpoint.py, and guard.py refactors with clear decision log entries

next_actions.action: Build structured JSONL event log at askr/state/events.jsonl recording trigger_fired, companion_spawned, session_ended events with session_id, parent_session_id, trigger_type, context_pct, context_tokens, quota_pct, project_path, timestamp
next_actions.why: Without it, overnight autonomous demo must be reverse-engineered from prose logs; with it, exact parent→child relationships and trigger metadata are directly queryable and defensible for the session tree visualization story

next_actions.action: Instrument lifecycle.py trigger firing sites (context, quota, idle) to emit events to structured log with parent_session_id captured from environment or checkpoint state
next_actions.why: Enables tracing which trigger type spawned each companion session and what resource state triggered it

next_actions.action: Instrument checkpoint.py companion spawn site to emit event with both session_id and parent_session_id
next_actions.why: Closes the lineage loop: records when a companion was created and by which parent session

next_actions.action: Pre-flight check: confirm daemon is running via launchd (askr launch), not just alive in a terminal
next_actions.why: Terminal daemons die on logout; launchd-registered daemon persists across reboots and is required for overnight autonomous run

next_actions.action: Pre-flight check: plug into power and run caffeinate if needed; seed goals.jsonl with at least one goal or hand-kick first task
next_actions.why: Ensures machine stays awake and daemon has work to trigger on during overnight run

next_actions.action: After overnight run completes, build visualization dashboard querying the structured event log to show session tree, context/quota savings, trigger type distribution, and multi-session persistence story
next_actions.why: Transforms raw event data into credible demo narrative for Sarvam hackathon registration and autonomous AI agent story

decisions.decision: Split quota notification into three phases: silent poll → user notification → reset wait
decisions.reason: Users working quickly were being interrupted at 90% threshold instead of real quota edge; silent polling lets them work to the genuine limit before notification

decisions.decision: Gitignore per-session scratch handovers and auto-delete them after checkpoint creation
decisions.reason: Scratch files were never cleaned up automatically and could accumulate indefinitely on disk or get committed; deleting immediately after merge prevents this

decisions.decision: Persist session_first_seen to disk like other trigger state (trigger_state, companioned_sessions)
decisions.reason: In-memory dict was reset on every daemon restart, causing grace period to reset for all active sessions and preventing triggers from ever firing

decisions.decision: Raise CONTEXT_TRIGGER from 0.60 to 0.70 and QUOTA_HIGH from 85 to 70
decisions.reason: Provides more runway before companion session opens; aligns thresholds across trigger types

decisions.decision: Exempt read-only Bash commands from cross-repo block guard while keeping write/edit commands blocked
decisions.reason: Allows diagnostic reads (cat, ls, tail, grep on sibling projects) while maintaining security against accidental writes

decisions.decision: Use shlex tokenization for Bash command parsing instead of raw regex split
decisions.reason: Honors quoted strings and prevents patterns like `grep -E 'pattern|with|pipes'` from being shattered into bogus fragments

decisions.decision: Build structured JSONL event log before overnight autonomous run to enable credible session lineage visualization
decisions.reason: Without it, the demo story must be reverse-engineered from prose logs; with it, the exact parent→child relationships and trigger metadata are directly queryable and defensible

relational_files.why: Trigger firing sites (context, quota, idle) need instrumentation to emit structured events
Companion spawn site needs event emission with session_id and parent_session_id linkage
May need to add event log path configuration and helper functions for structured event emission
Read-only Bash command exemption logic now in place; no further changes needed

blockers: Structured JSONL event log not yet built; without it, overnight autonomous run visualization cannot credibly show session lineage and trigger metadata
